description: Letter, opening with biblical quotations, including Psalms 16:8, 34:8 and 33:21, and mentioning commodities such as silk. (Information from CUDL.) The letter is addressed to a father. It is headed by the unusual formula בשמך רחמנא וחננא. Much of the text is in elegant rhymed prose, which along with the handwriting and the mention of the sulṭān (v8), suggests that this could be a letter from the physician Abū Zikrī to his father Eliyyahu the Judge in the early 13th century (cf. T-S 24.72). Might have to do with the granting of a governmental honor/employment; after the mention of the sulṭān quotes somebody saying 'yes, we will honor him' (naʿam nusharrifuh). Very faded and needs examination. ASE
description_he: מכתב הפותח בציטוטים מתהלים טז:8, לד:8 ו-לג:21, ומזכיר סחורות כגון משי. מופנה לאב עם כותרת 'בשמך רחמנא וחננא'. חלקים בפרוזה מחורזת. ייתכן שנכתב על ידי הרופא אבו זיכרי לאביו אליהו הדיין (תחילת המאה ה-13). מתייחס כנראה להענקת כבוד ממשלתי.
